title: myosotis palustris subsp. multiflora
intro: Myosotis palustris subsp. multiflora, commonly known as the marsh forget-me-not, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Myosotis palustris subsp. multiflora include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
